inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
irony: noun. The quality of a statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is
minor: adjective. Lesser, smaller in importance, size, degree, seriousness, or significance compared to another option, particularly:
minority: noun. The state or condition of being smaller, inferior, or subordinate to something or someone else; lesserness.
mint: noun. A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
minty: adjective. Having a flavor or essence of mint.
mir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isthmus Mixe.
miry: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a mire; swampy, boggy.
mity: adjective. Alternative spelling of mitey (“infested with mites”).
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mort: noun. Death; especially, the death of game in hunting.
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
nitro: noun. The univalent NO 2 functional group.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
norm: noun. That which is normal or typical.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
omit: verb. To leave out or exclude.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
rim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nyaturu.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
torii: noun. A traditional Japanese gate at Shinto shrines, symbolically marking the transition from the profane to the sacred.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
trim: verb. To reduce slightly; to cut; especially, to remove excess.
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
try: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Turung.
yin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yinchia.
yom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yombe.
yon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yongkom.
